title: On Windows 11 Home, Initializing a Podman machine failed
body:
### Bug description

On Windows 11 Home, Initializing a Podman machine failed


### Operating system

Windows 11 Home

### Version

0.15.0

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install Podman Desktop 0.15.0
2. Install Podman from Podman Desktop
3. Initialize Podman

### Relevant log output


```
The operation timed out because a response was not received from the virtual machine or container. Error code: Wsl/Service/CreateVM/HCS_E_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT
Errot: the WSL import of guest OS failed: exit status 0xffffffff
```

### Additional context

![2023-05-09-Selection-937pxx177px-01](https://github.com/containers/podman-desktop/assets/243761/5eca95bc-0eed-4908-b44c-1cf36e3d328c)

comments.body: moving to podman repository as it's just a call to `podman machine init`, Podman Desktop code is not involved
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

You probably need to update your OS and WSL version to resolve this. To do this run Windows Update and apply all updates. After rebooting run it again and accept the major enhancement updates as well. You maybe have to do this multiple times, with the goal of getting to 22H2 and all current updates applied on top of that. Once Windows update stops suggesting updates, confirm that you are on 22H2 by running the winver command (it will be listed towards the top of the dialogue). After that run wsl —update on the command prompt, which should install the new Windows Store based version (reboot likely required). To confirm this was successful you should see a bunch of versions with the wsl —version command. If you don’t, and instead get help text, try wsl —update again. Once you are running a version that does output version info, then run “podman machine init” to recreate the VM (you may need to run podman machine rm to clear out state from the previous failed install)
